module_mood_contract: earn the trust — M3 mood; this exercise is multi-session-as-packaging infrastructure (§57), not a teaching beat itself.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named; mood-floor 8+/10 applies to TEACHING beats only.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Single phase is a setup beat (multi-session positioning, no teaching). Diagnostic met: the exercise could compress to a parenthetical in the M3 arc without breaking the arc. Mood-floor exempt; record mood_score:null and judge on clarity + brevity. Body is concise (~30 lines), no extraneous prose, lane discipline lands.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — don't front-run a downstream teaching moment.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc M3 names S-first then Q, three exercises, Ex3 authors the test-strategy skill at ~/.claude/skills/test-strategy/SKILL.md (user scope, auto-discovered in every session). The file's maintainer block (L42) accurately states the skill installs to user scope and crosses worktrees freely; body (L23) names that 'Exercise 3 returns to the main lane to invoke on the security-tested feature' — consistent with the strategy's S-then-Q-consuming-S sequencing. No teaching moment from Ex1/Ex2/Ex3 is pre-empted; the file only establishes the two-window container.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Multi-session ships as packaging, not as a new taught primitive.
rules_evaluated.evidence: This file is the canonical case named in §57. The container (two worktrees, parallel sessions) is set up at module open; existing Ex1/Ex2/Ex3 each carry a one-line window callout. Diagnostic met: removing the container would still leave Ex1/Ex2/Ex3 teachable (Q+S could run sequentially); the container is packaging, not a new primitive. Per-moment cognitive load stays single-task.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Two mandatory teaching prompts (`ae101-m3-fork-quality-side` at L17, `ae101-m3-quality-side-orient` at L25) carry the forcing functions — fork the worktree on a fresh branch, copy personal files, confirm side-lane state. Body prose around them is artifact-contract framing (what the windows are for, lane discipline at L27) — not a forcing function in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; no authored cohort-management conversations.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'discuss with your neighbor', 'share with the room', or pause-cue language in body. Every sentence names the work (open a session, paste the prompt, lane discipline, what happened).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pre-action framing blocks don't preview the prompt's payoff.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 'What happened' (L29) is in POST-action position — after both prompts have run. Names the terminal artifact (two sessions open, security picks up at next exercise), not the mechanism. Pre-action body around prompts names activity + constraint (L13 'decide where to base...', L15 'Paste the prompt as-is. Claude reads...') — both KEEP categories per the three-axis diagnostic.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: `/clear` is a tool with a purpose, not a ceremony.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No `/clear` in this opener. Consistent with §58: this is a session-start beat, no convergence step needed.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block L61–L64 names three falsifiable verbs: opens a sibling worktree from a chosen branch when two angles shouldn't share a scrollback; runs two Claude Code sessions side by side on the same git history, one window per worktree; routes work between windows by lane/window callout rather than tab-switching mid-prompt. All three are observable on the engineer's own codebase by Monday.

Maintainer block 'Push-back moves' (L48–L51) names three dominant failures + recovery moves: same-cwd both sessions (trainer/Nerd line + source), student opens shell to run `git worktree add` themselves (redirect to conversation), forgets to copy personal files (recovery: ask Claude in side lane to copy from main repo).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No lecture content in this file.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: One-sentence action lead-in before each prompt block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Fork prompt: body L15 ('Paste the prompt as-is. Claude reads `<repo-name>` as your repo's folder name from the working directory.') sits immediately above the marker at L17 — short, semantic, names what the agent does with the paste. Orient prompt: body L23 ('Start a second Claude Code session... in the sibling worktree directory Claude just named. This is your side-quest lane.') leads the marker at L25 — names the session-start action and the lane assignment.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body lead-in mirrors the prompt's opening phrasing.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Fork: body L13–L15 names 'firing the fork' + 'Paste the prompt as-is'; prompt opens 'Fork a sibling worktree' — verb + noun rhyme. Orient: body L23 names 'Start a second Claude Code session... in the sibling worktree directory Claude just named'; prompt opens 'Confirm worktree state' — less verbatim mirror but the body's 'second session in the sibling worktree directory' establishes the noun-phrase the prompt confirms; reads as one continuous sentence-shape. Light, not a violation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Prompts live in registry; module/exercise files reference them by marker.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Both prompts referenced by `{{prompt:<key>}}` markers (L17, L25). Registry entries exist at `curriculum/prompts/ae101-m3-fork-quality-side.md` and `curriculum/prompts/ae101-m3-quality-side-orient.md` with full frontmatter (key, dest, runtime, origin, requires, produces).
